questions.question: You run a plumbing business and want to automate quoting and job scheduling. Which tool would be most relevant?

answers.feedback: Tradify is the best fit here. It's specifically built for trades businesses like plumbers, electricians, and builders, with AI-powered quoting and scheduling.
That's right. Tradify is purpose-built for trades businesses — it handles quoting, job scheduling, invoicing, and timesheets with AI-assisted features.
Tradify is the best fit here. It's specifically built for trades businesses like plumbers, electricians, and builders, with AI-powered quoting and scheduling.

questions.question: Your business already uses Google Workspace (Gmail, Docs, Sheets). Which AI assistant is the most cost-effective starting point?

answers.feedback: Gemini is the best choice here. Since you're already on Google Workspace, Gemini integrates directly with your existing tools and may already be included in your subscription.
Gemini is the best choice here. Since you're already on Google Workspace, Gemini integrates directly with your existing tools and may already be included in your subscription.
Spot on. Gemini is built into Google Workspace and may already be included in your plan at $14/user/month — making it the most cost-effective option if you're already in the Google ecosystem.
Gemini is the best choice here. Since you're already on Google Workspace, Gemini integrates directly with your existing tools and may already be included in your subscription.

questions.question: Which of the following are AI features included in Xero at no extra charge? (Select all that apply)

answers.feedback: Well done. Xero's JAX AI includes auto-categorisation, bank reconciliation suggestions, and cash flow forecasting — all included in your existing subscription.
Xero's JAX AI includes auto-categorisation of expenses, bank reconciliation suggestions, and cash flow forecasting. It doesn't handle marketing emails or social media — those are separate tools.

questions.question: A Wellington hotel wants to handle after-hours booking enquiries without hiring night staff. Based on the NZ examples in this module, what approach has worked?

answers.text: Hiring a virtual assistant in a different time zone
answers.feedback: The Bolton Hotel in Wellington deployed an AI chatbot called Bea that handles booking enquiries 24/7. It's a proven NZ example of AI solving the after-hours customer service problem.

answers.text: Deploying an AI chatbot like the Bolton Hotel's 'Bea'
answers.feedback: Correct. The Bolton Hotel's chatbot Bea handles booking enquiries, room information, and local recommendations 24/7 without additional staffing costs.
The Bolton Hotel in Wellington deployed an AI chatbot called Bea that handles booking enquiries 24/7. It's a proven NZ example of AI solving the after-hours customer service problem.

answers.text: Redirecting all enquiries to a call centre

questions.question: What percentage of customer inquiries can AI chatbots like Tidio's Lyro handle without human involvement?

answers.feedback: Tidio's Lyro AI can handle up to 64% of customer inquiries without human involvement. That's a significant chunk of routine questions handled automatically.
Tidio's Lyro AI can handle up to 64% of customer inquiries without human involvement. That's a significant chunk of routine questions handled automatically.

questions.question: When should you keep a human in the loop rather than fully automating with AI? (Select all that apply)

answers.feedback: Exactly. Customer complaints, hiring decisions, and high-value sales all require human judgment, empathy, and personal relationships. Receipt processing and order confirmations are ideal candidates for full automation.

Tasks requiring judgment, personal relationships, or where mistakes have serious consequences — like complaints, hiring, and high-value sales — should keep humans involved. Repetitive, rule-based tasks like receipt processing and order confirmations are safe to fully automate.

Exactly. Customer complaints, hiring decisions, and high-value sales all require human judgment, empathy, and personal relationships. Receipt processing and order confirmations are ideal candidates for full automation.

Tasks requiring judgment, personal relationships, or where mistakes have serious consequences — like complaints, hiring, and high-value sales — should keep humans involved. Repetitive, rule-based tasks like receipt processing and order confirmations are safe to fully automate.

Exactly. Customer complaints, hiring decisions, and high-value sales all require human judgment, empathy, and personal relationships. Receipt processing and order confirmations are ideal candidates for full automation.

questions.question: You're a sole trader and want to automate your tax calculations and GST payments. Which NZ tool is specifically designed for this?

answers.feedback: Hnry is the tool built specifically for this. It's designed for NZ sole traders and automatically handles income tax, GST, ACC, and student loan deductions at just 1% of income (capped at $1,500/year). Xero is great accounting software but doesn't automate tax payments the same way.

That's right. Hnry is specifically built for NZ sole traders, contractors, and freelancers. It automatically calculates and pays income tax, GST, ACC, and student loan with every payment you receive — at just 1% of income, capped at $1,500/year.

Hnry is the tool built specifically for this. It's designed for NZ sole traders and automatically handles income tax, GST, ACC, and student loan deductions at just 1% of income (capped at $1,500/year). Xero is great accounting software but doesn't automate tax payments the same way.

questions.question: According to the AI Forum of NZ, what is the average return on investment for AI tools in New Zealand businesses?

answers.feedback: The AI Forum of NZ reports an average ROI of 3.7x, which works out to $3.50 return for every $1 invested. That's a strong return, especially given that 75% of organisations found their setup costs were under $5,000.

The AI Forum of NZ reports an average ROI of 3.7x, which works out to $3.50 return for every $1 invested. That's a strong return, especially given that 75% of organisations found their setup costs were under $5,000.

Correct. The AI Forum reports an average ROI of 3.7x — meaning $3.50 back for every $1 invested. Combined with the fact that 75% of organisations found setup costs under $5,000, AI is proving to be a solid investment for NZ businesses.
